prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(initialize_workflow_instance primary_participant_instance)
(allocate_workflow_resource primary_participant_instance resource_token_a)
(bind_operation_type_to_workflow primary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(confirm_workflow_operation_binding primary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(attach_primary_artifact primary_participant_instance signal_channel_alpha supporting_artifact)
(restore_primary_artifact primary_participant_instance signal_channel_alpha standard_operation_type supporting_artifact)
(release_operation_type_binding primary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(initialize_workflow_instance secondary_participant_instance)
(allocate_workflow_resource secondary_participant_instance resource_token_b)
(bind_operation_type_to_workflow secondary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(confirm_workflow_operation_binding secondary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(attach_secondary_artifact secondary_participant_instance signal_channel_beta supporting_artifact)
(restore_secondary_artifact secondary_participant_instance signal_channel_beta standard_operation_type supporting_artifact)
(release_operation_type_binding secondary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(consolidate_output_bundle_variant_a primary_participant_instance secondary_participant_instance signal_channel_alpha signal_channel_beta consolidated_output_bundle)
(bind_operation_type_to_workflow primary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(verify_output_bundle consolidated_output_bundle primary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(mark_primary_participant_complete primary_participant_instance consolidated_output_bundle)
(mark_secondary_participant_complete secondary_participant_instance consolidated_output_bundle)
(release_operation_type_binding primary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(initialize_workflow_instance lead_orchestrator)
(allocate_workflow_resource lead_orchestrator resource_token_c)
(bind_operation_type_to_workflow lead_orchestrator standard_operation_type)
(confirm_workflow_operation_binding lead_orchestrator standard_operation_type)
(bind_execution_parameter_to_orchestrator lead_orchestrator execution_parameter_main)
(bind_notification_template_to_orchestrator lead_orchestrator notification_template_main)
(bind_capability_to_workflow lead_orchestrator processing_capability)
(bind_attachment_to_orchestrator lead_orchestrator attachment_a consolidated_output_bundle)
(certify_attachment_binding lead_orchestrator attachment_a consolidated_output_bundle standard_operation_type)
(promote_orchestrator_checkpoint_a lead_orchestrator execution_parameter_main processing_capability attachment_a consolidated_output_bundle)
(promote_orchestrator_checkpoint_c lead_orchestrator notification_template_main attachment_a consolidated_output_bundle)
(seal_orchestrator_finalization lead_orchestrator)
(bind_audit_token_to_workflow_instance lead_orchestrator audit_token_main standard_operation_type)
(finalize_orchestrator lead_orchestrator resource_token_c audit_token_main)
(release_operation_type_binding lead_orchestrator standard_operation_type)
(release_capability_binding lead_orchestrator processing_capability)
(unbind_execution_parameter_from_orchestrator lead_orchestrator execution_parameter_main)
(unbind_notification_template_from_orchestrator lead_orchestrator notification_template_main)
(initialize_workflow_instance support_orchestrator)
(allocate_workflow_resource support_orchestrator resource_token_c)
(bind_operation_type_to_workflow support_orchestrator standard_operation_type)
(confirm_workflow_operation_binding support_orchestrator standard_operation_type)
(bind_execution_parameter_to_orchestrator support_orchestrator execution_parameter_main)
(bind_notification_template_to_orchestrator support_orchestrator notification_template_main)
(bind_capability_to_workflow support_orchestrator processing_capability)
(bind_attachment_to_orchestrator support_orchestrator attachment_b consolidated_output_bundle)
(certify_attachment_binding support_orchestrator attachment_b consolidated_output_bundle standard_operation_type)
(promote_orchestrator_checkpoint_a support_orchestrator execution_parameter_main processing_capability attachment_b consolidated_output_bundle)
(promote_orchestrator_checkpoint_c support_orchestrator notification_template_main attachment_b consolidated_output_bundle)
(seal_orchestrator_finalization support_orchestrator)
(bind_audit_token_to_workflow_instance support_orchestrator audit_token_main standard_operation_type)
(finalize_orchestrator support_orchestrator resource_token_c audit_token_main)
(release_operation_type_binding support_orchestrator standard_operation_type)
(release_capability_binding support_orchestrator processing_capability)
(unbind_execution_parameter_from_orchestrator support_orchestrator execution_parameter_main)
(unbind_notification_template_from_orchestrator support_orchestrator notification_template_main)
(bind_operation_type_to_workflow primary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(bind_audit_token_to_workflow_instance primary_participant_instance audit_token_main standard_operation_type)
(finalize_primary_participant primary_participant_instance resource_token_a audit_token_main)
(release_operation_type_binding primary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(bind_operation_type_to_workflow secondary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(bind_audit_token_to_workflow_instance secondary_participant_instance audit_token_main standard_operation_type)
(finalize_secondary_participant secondary_participant_instance resource_token_b audit_token_main)
message.reasoning:
The problem involves a multi-step workflow with primary_participant_instance, secondary_participant_instance, lead_orchestrator, and support_orchestrator. The goal is to finalize all of them.


## Examining Workflow Components  
- The problem includes a multi-step workflow involving key roles: primary_participant_instance, secondary_participant_instance, lead_orchestrator, and support_orchestrator.  
- The goal is to finalize all components, ensuring each role is clearly defined and integrated.

## Examining Workflow Components  
- All instances—primary_participant, secondary_participant, lead_orchestrator, and support_orchestrator—are treated as workflow_instances, with types participant_group_a for participants and participant_group_b for orchestrators.  
- Initial state includes available bindings like channels and links, setting the stage for further actions.  

## Identifying Finalization Steps  
- Finalizing each instance requires passing through audit_stage_open, which needs completion_signaled and specific bindings like workflow_resource_binding.  
- Completion_signal can be triggered by actions like mark_primary_participant_complete for participants or orchestrator_finalized for orchestrators, which has multiple pathways like seal_orchestrator_finalization.  

## Analyzing Orchestrator Differences  
- Lead_orchestrator links to approval_reference_a, which is initially available, while support_orchestrator links to approval_reference_b, which isn’t available at the start.  
- Both orchestrators share the same primary, secondary, and output_bundle but have different attachments, complicating the finalization process.  

## Planning Finalization Sequence  
- The goal is to find a sequence that finalizes all four instances, processing participants first, then consolidating the bundle, and handling orchestrators separately.  
- Actions are designed for single instances, requiring individual resource bindings for each, despite shared elements.

## Planning Finalization Sequence  
- To finalize all instances, process participants first by initializing and binding resources, then move to orchestrators.  
- Actions like initialize_workflow_instance and allocate_workflow_resource are required for each instance, suggesting a sequential approach due to shared resources.  

## Outlining Participant Finalization  
- For participants like primary_participant_instance, mark_primary_participant_complete requires primed, committed, output_prepared, and verified states to set completion_signaled.  
- Finalizing participants needs audit_stage_open, which involves binding audit with operation and ensuring resource_binding, like primary_channel_binding.  

## Detailing Orchestrator Processes  
- Orchestrators follow a review cycle: authorize_orchestrator_capability sets review_confirmed after review_started, leading to review_closed via confirm_orchestrator_review.  
- Complete_orchestrator_finalization then sets finalized and completion_signaled, but support_orchestrator faces challenges as approval_reference_b isn’t initially available.  

## Addressing Resource Constraints  
- Both orchestrators share resources like operation_type, capability, and output_bundle, requiring careful sequencing to avoid conflicts.  
- Verify_output_bundle needs primary_participant with operation_binding, highlighting the need to process participants before orchestrators.

## Sequencing Participant Actions  
- For primary participant, first attach artifact to set buffered and primed states, then restore to emit signal and commit, freeing the artifact.  
- Secondary participant follows, but requires releasing the operation from primary first, ensuring sequential binding and freeing for both.  

## Managing Artifact and Operation Binding  
- Attach_artifact for both primary and secondary sets buffered and primed without committing, requiring context_enabled from prior operation confirmation.  
- Restore_artifact needs operation binding, channel, and buffered state, setting emitted, committed, and freeing artifact, ensuring sequence aligns with shared resource availability.  

## Planning Consolidation Process  
- Both participants need to be primed, committed, and emitted for variant_a consolidation, achieved by sequencing attach and restore actions.  
- Consolidation requires releasing operation bindings post-secondary to ensure output_bundle is prepared, linking primary and secondary outputs.

## Extending Consolidation Process  
- Variant_a consolidation doesn't set bundle markers, using certify_attachment_binding instead of certify_policy_binding due to missing marker_a.  
- Promotion from stage_a to stage_b uses promote_a, requiring parameter and capability binding, but skips marker_b, setting stage_b without markers.  

## Advancing Orchestration Stages  
- From stage_b, promote to stage_c via promote_orchestrator_checkpoint_c, relying on notification links and attachments, setting stage_c without markers.  
- Sealing finalization at stage_c sets finalized and completion_signaled, requiring parameter, capability, and notification bindings, bypassing enrichment_ready.  

## Sequencing Lead Orchestrator  
- Initialize and allocate resources for lead_orchestrator, binding operation type and confirming context_enabled for lead.  
- Bind execution parameter, notification, and capability to lead_orchestrator, ensuring context_enabled and resource availability for subsequent steps.

## Handling Support Orchestrator  
- Process support_orchestrator similarly to lead, using attachment_b, which is available and verified.  
- Unbind parameters, notifications, and capabilities from lead to make them available for support_orchestrator.  

## Binding Operations for Support  
- Initialize and allocate resources for support_orchestrator, binding operation type and confirming context_enabled.  
- Bind execution parameter, notification, and capability to support, ensuring attachments and bundles are prepared.  

## Finalizing Support Orchestrator  
- Certify attachment binding and promote checkpoints for support_orchestrator, skipping markers, then seal finalization.  
- Bind audit token to support_orchestrator, finalizing it and releasing resources, setting audit links and completion signaled.  

## Managing Participant Finalization  
- Bind operation to primary participant, ensuring workflow is open and resources allocated, then bind audit token.  
- Finalize primary participant, releasing resources and audit, then repeat for secondary participant after releasing operations.

## Orchestrating Lead Processes  
- Bind attachment_a to lead_orchestrator with consolidated_output_bundle, certifying the attachment binding.  
- Promote checkpoints for lead_orchestrator, using execution_parameter_main and notification_template_main, then seal finalization.  

## Finalizing Lead Operations  
- Bind audit token to lead_orchestrator, finalize using resource_token_c, then release operation, capability, parameter, and notification bindings.  
- Ensure all resources are available for subsequent orchestrator processes.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
